rationale: Agrawal et al. (2007) is cited in the conceptual development to support a specific claim that emotion-message fit (including self/other framing) improves fluency and message effectiveness. The citing paper then explicitly builds on this fit logic to derive its own boundary-condition hypotheses for awe-based limited-edition promotions. Although grouped with related citations, the role of this paper is clear and meaningful in framing the theoretical extension.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Agrawal, Menon, and Aaker (2007) as prior evidence on emotion-message congruence and extends that logic to a new context: awe in limited-edition product advertising. It tests whether congruent connection-oriented or cause-related cues can offset or reverse awe's typical negative effect on limited-edition preferences.
